programme.title: What on Earth?: Monsters of Diamond Desert
programme.desc: An abandoned ghost town in a remote desert with a dark past. When a strange facility is spotted in Russia, experts investigate if it's hiding evidence of alien life.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Lost City of Goliath
programme.desc: A lost city near Jerusalem might have been the home of Goliath. Bizarre underwater formations might point to a top-secret US weapons testing facility. A strange sighting in the Arctic could be a lost Nazi relic or the icy remains of prehistoric monsters.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Ruins of Cannibal City
programme.desc: When an extreme drought hits Spain, the ruins of a long-lost civilization suddenly emerge, and using cutting-edge technology, experts investigate evidence of ancient cannibalism.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Cannibal Death Pit
programme.desc: A strange circular forest is found next to a notorious cannibal site. Then, when an eerie crater is spotted in Siberia, experts uncover if it could be evidence of Nikola Tesla's doomsday device.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Bermuda Triangle of Space
programme.desc: When instruments on the International Space Station suddenly malfunction every time it passes over a mysterious site in the Atlantic Ocean, experts investigate if this eerie behaviours is more than just a tech glitch, but a prophecy of global doomsday.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Aliens of the Infinity War
programme.desc: Experts wonder if a strange pattern that appears in a field is an extraterrestrial response to a message sent to an alien star cluster in 1974, and using cutting-edge science, they uncover its true origin.

programme.title: What on Earth?: America's Swamp Castle
programme.desc: Satellite images capture bizarre structures in the remote and inhospitable swamplands of Louisiana. Experts investigate a forgotten war-torn fortress with a mysterious connection to the sinking ruins of a classified military installation.

programme.title: The Face of Evil: Seeds of Death
programme.desc: When a young woman is found shot to death near a small Alaskan village, detectives rely on forensic science and to catch her murderer. Then an eyewitness comes forward with an explosive revelation that threatens to tear apart the small town.

programme.title: Murder Decoded: Footsteps in Blood
programme.desc: A man calls 911 when he finds blood inside a friend's home. Officers enter to find a beloved community activist shot dead. Detectives piece together clues bloody footprints, bullet fragments, a credit card trail to find a cold-blooded murderer.

programme.title: Murder Calls: The Slender Man
programme.desc: When a young mother is shot dead in front of her son, horrified witnesses call 911 and describe the shooter as a slender man who disappeared into nearby trees. Though clues are scarce, investigators finally uncover the killer's shocking identity.

programme.title: Dead Silent: Quiet the Dogs
programme.desc: After Connie Hilton and her husband George leave Dallas and move to a rural enclave in East Texas, life couldn't be more perfect. But one early September morning, their peaceful, easy-living country life is abruptly and brutally shattered for good.

programme.title: 48 Hours: The Corn Rake Murder
programme.desc: Todd Mullis found his wife Amy dead in their barn, a corn rake sticking in her back and claims it was a freak accident. Todd has an alibi; his 13-year-old son was working with him on the farm that day.

programme.title: See No Evil: Headlights and Fire
programme.desc: In Pierce County, Washington, a woman's body is found set on fire beside a dead-end road. Detectives struggle to identify their victim, until cameras lead to a shocking discovery at the woman's home and put them on the trail of a cold-blooded killer.

programme.title: The Murder Tapes: The Last Laugh
programme.desc: Dawn Sandoval is gone. What appears to be a missing person’s case, take a stunning turn when very different portraits of her are revealed. Now, detectives must weed through a series of tangled relationships to uncover where Dawn has been all along.

programme.title: 48 Hours: The Corn Rake Murder
programme.desc: Todd Mullis found his wife Amy dead in their barn, a corn rake sticking in her back and claims it was a freak accident. Todd has an alibi; his 13-year-old son was working with him on the farm that day.

programme.title: See No Evil: Headlights and Fire
programme.desc: In Pierce County, Washington, a woman's body is found set on fire beside a dead-end road. Detectives struggle to identify their victim, until cameras lead to a shocking discovery at the woman's home and put them on the trail of a cold-blooded killer.

programme.title: The Murder Tapes: He's Not Going to Stop
programme.desc: Dr. Stephen Pitt, Veleria Sharp, Laura Anderson, Marshall Levine, Bryon Thomas and Mary Simmons are dead. Now, bodycam video captures the hunt of the spree shooter as he stays one step ahead. Before it's over, a shootout uncovers a raging madman.

programme.title: Dead Silent: Strange Passenger
programme.desc: In May 2000, friends Liz Reiser and Brandi Hicks go to a video store in the quaint Ohio town of New Philadelphia. Their plans for an innocent movie night morph into a true-life horror film when they give a ride to a stranger in the parking lot.

programme.title: Dead of Night: He Shouldn't Be With Us
programme.desc: A woman's attempt to flee a troubled relationship leads to her demise. Police discover a haunting message left behind, but the case goes cold for years until a prime suspect's macabre desire for blood is revealed.

programme.title: Dead of Night: That's My Brother
programme.desc: Two murders just one year apart have the police of Great Falls, Montana on high alert. Morris Davis and Suzette Pritchard are the victims of brutal overkill, leading police to believe there is a killer is on the loose. But one tip changes everything.

programme.title: Swamp Murders: Point of No Return
programme.desc: When shots ring out on a remote Florida beach, officers are devastated to learn the lives of two Georgia teens have been lost. Even with two jurisdictions on the case, leads are scarce. Until an unexpected call comes in and promises swift resolution.

programme.title: The Interrogator: Overkill
programme.desc: A young woman finds her beloved neighbour, Rebecca Claybrook, beaten to death in her own home. The men in Claybrook's life are put under the microscope by Detective Fil Waters.

programme.title: Suspicious Minds: In Case of Murder
programme.desc: The untimely death of Melissa Henderson, wife and mother of two, rattles a small Indiana town -- but revelations of foul play at her funeral set the rumor mill ablaze. Local gossip leads investigators to a riverboat casino with unexpected suspects.

programme.title: Killer in Question: The Man with the Rabbit's Foot
programme.desc: When police find three people killed execution-style in the cooler of a Louisiana convenience store, it's just the start of the mystery. One man will give a series of conflicting confessions, leaving everyone to wonder what really happened that night.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Find Jodi
programme.desc: More than two decades after an Iowa TV anchor disappeared, '48 Hours' reveals new information into the investigation.

programme.title: 48 Hours: What Happened in the Sutton Place Apartment?
programme.desc: It's a story of wealth, privilege, and an early morning after-party that went terribly wrong. Erin Moriarty explores the last night of Joey Comunale's life and the cases against two privileged young men for taking part in a murder.

programme.title: Forbidden: Dying for Love: Together Forever, Forever Together
programme.desc: A young teen forbidden to date falls in love with a senior at her high school. The two go to extreme lengths to keep their relationship secret but when it is threatened with exposure they vow to stay together, whatever the cost.

programme.title: 'Til Death Do Us Part: In Sickness and Health
programme.desc: Cynthia Smith and David Mueller couldn't be more different, but they say opposites attract. When these two meet, there's an instant connection.

programme.title: Fatal Vows: Bloody Matrimony
programme.desc: Steve Dingle, a young Filipino immigrant, returns to his homeland to find a bride. It seems like a match made in heaven when he meets Cathy. But pressure builds in their marriage that will explode into infidelity, violence, and murder.

programme.title: Primal Instinct: From Diamonds to Death
programme.desc: Ann McDonald's wealthy husband Rich cares more about social status than her happiness. When she seeks solace in Al Braunberger's arms, it unleashes a side of Rich she's never seen and a side of Al she never expected, trapping her in a deadly game.

programme.title: UFOs: Uncovering the Truth: Ancient UFO Earth Landings
programme.desc: There's new evidence pointing to the possibility that ancient man was visited by extraterrestrials, influencing ancient technologies, architecture and art. One scholar reveals evidence supporting his theory that ancient Sumerians were created by aliens.

programme.title: UFOs: Uncovering the Truth: Pilots and Astronauts
programme.desc: Through amazing first-hand accounts from pilots and astronauts, we'll learn that sightings of mysterious, unknown objects in our skies are far more common than we're lead to believe.

programme.title: UFOs: Uncovering the Truth: Past and Present
programme.desc: Many researchers believe we have been visited by otherworldly spacecraft since the dawn of man. Trace the evidence from the history of ancient Egypt, to the myths of Native Americans, and through the art of the Renaissance period.

programme.title: Mafia Drug King: Carmine Galante
programme.desc: The story of Carmine Galante, who made money from selling heroin, and still profited from his drug empire while locked in prison.

programme.title: The Renegade Mobster
programme.desc: Crazy Joey Gallo psychopathic killer, beatnik hipster and renegade mobster. Burning with rage and determined to make his way to the top, he wages all-out war against the US and sparks a revolution in the mafia before going down in a hail of bullets.

programme.title: Al Capone: Public Enemy #1
programme.desc: America's first superstar gangster, Al Capone, dominated headlines and is said to be responsible for the bloody St Valentines' Day Massacre. After being made the face of organized crime by US government Capone's public image brings about his downfall.

programme.title: The Mexican Godfather
programme.desc: The hideously mutilated body of a DEA agent sparks the largest manhunt in the Drug Enforcement Administration's history. It would take a special unit of investigators, and a battalion of Mexican soldiers to bring the killers to justice.

programme.title: American Lawmen: Melvin Purvis: The Gangbuster
programme.desc: During the Great Depression, a violent crime wave is sweeping the nation. It was up to the FBI's Melvin Purvis to lead a massive manhunt for the most dangerous men in America.

programme.title: Gunslingers: Wild Bill: Marksman and Marked Man
programme.desc: When the most famous man in the Old West outlives his legend, he becomes the target of gunslingers eager to make legends of their own by shooting him dead.

programme.title: Legends of the West: Deacon Jim Miller: The Pious Assassin
programme.desc: His neighbours saw a devout family man. The last thing his victims saw was a shotgun aimed at their heads. If there's an outlaw who was born to kill it's Deacon Jim Miller, the Bible-thumping psycho-killer.

programme.title: Gunslingers: Tom Horn: Grim Reaper of the Rockies
programme.desc: As the Wild West becomes settled, this mysterious hit man kills for men who want to preserve the old order where violence rules all. But the law isn't giving in this time.

programme.title: Legends of the West: Bass Reeves: The Real Lone Ranger
programme.desc: Bass Reeves is no ordinary lawman. A slave set free by the Civil War, he hunts outlaws in the most dangerous territory in the nation. There is striking evidence that this fearless, legendary rider is the basis for the iconic character of the Lone Ranger.

programme.title: Gunslingers: Billy the Kid: The Phantom of Lincoln County
programme.desc: On a bloodstained frontier where corruption is king, an unlikely hero becomes a crusader for justice and revenge. By the time of his death at the age of 21, everyone in the country had heard of Billy the Kid.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston: Coastal Cottage

programme.desc: Clay and Clayton were thrilled to negotiate their house price lower based on the issues raised in their home inspection report. But now that they've put thousands of dollars and hours into repairs, they're discovering things the home inspector missed.

programme.title: Paid Program
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: Maryland Strip Mall
programme.desc: The crew returns to Largo, MD, where they harvest items from a sporting goods store at the Boulevard at the Capital Centre. Robert and Grayson salvage the electrical system while Mike, Tay and Ted remove exterior beams for a future cantilever bench.

programme.title: Paid Program
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Family Fishing Cabin
programme.desc: The crew work to save an old cabin in Oakland, Maine, that Eric purchased from his father. He hopes the renovation will give the cabin many more years of life so he can shire his childhood memories there with his own children.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: The Morrill Family Bunkhouse
programme.desc: Chase Morrill and his team of builders take on the old army barracks on their family camp that has served as overflow sleeping for over 40 years.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Family's Empty Nest
programme.desc: A family has the resources to give their Lincolnville, Maine, cabin the love it deserves now that their four children are almost done with college. However the cabin has been neglected for 25 years and corners were cut in the original build.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: A Family Cabin Fit for a King
programme.desc: The team has $50,000 to renovate a camp that's four-times the size their usual builds. After a series of delays, the crew needs an extension on their deadline. The cabin gets a complete exterior and interior facelift including an outdoor checkerboard.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: A Family Gathering Place
programme.desc: Chase and his team take on a project on McGrath Pond in Oakland, ME, that has been empty for 15 years. Stephanie bought this place from her father and promised him she would fix it up.

programme.title: Pool Kings: Canyon Country Playground
programme.desc: A big family on a large property wants to replace their old patio and pool with an updated design. Mike and Jim deliver on the dream by transforming the yard into a canyon country playground. The backyard oasis includes a lazy river and a cave.

programme.title: Pool Kings: Vegas Jackpot Swimming Pool
programme.desc: A Las Vegas family hits the jackpot when Paul and Brian come to design the pool of their dreams. The guys are put to the test to turn the narrow desert yard into a world of water that includes splash pads, an outdoor kitchen and a pro volleyball net.

programme.title: Pool Kings: Lake View Paradise
programme.desc: A couple wants to build their daughter a backyard oasis that also accentuates their beautiful lake views.

programme.title: Pool Kings: Mountain Lake Resort Pool
programme.desc: An active family in southern California wants an extravagant pool to remind them of their favorite lakeside vacations. The kings don't know which is bigger, their clients' wish list or the 20-foot jumping cliff they have to create from scratch.

programme.title: Pool Kings: I'm on Vacation Already
programme.desc: Now that the interior of their dream house is finished, a family of five asks the Pool Kings to transform their neglected backyard. They want a relaxing multi-level private resort with a waterfall grotto, huge spa, swim-up bar and outdoor living room.

programme.title: Pool Kings: Cancun in Every Corner
programme.desc: The kings pull out all the stops to maximize an Orange County family's backyard. The shady and dated space is transformed into a three-tiered vacation paradise the family can enjoy at home.

programme.title: Pool Kings: Pool Party Paradise
programme.desc: Tony and Paul help a couple build their dream pool to go along with their newly built outdoor entertaining space. The guys are challenged to design around a giant concrete slab by creating a feature-packed pool that fits with the new space.

programme.title: Pool Kings: A Rockin' Resort Pool
programme.desc: The Pool Kings go above and beyond to create a project that has space for everyone in a big family. It includes a 15-foot tall natural rock grotto, swim-up bar, sun shelf and outdoor living room.

programme.title: Pool Kings: Desert Oasis in Sin City
programme.desc: Mike Baldasare and Jim Bellamy, two best friends that build award winning pools for clients all across the southwest, head to Las Vegas to build a dream pool for Shane and Willow Jager's brand new dream home.

programme.title: Pool Kings: Cliffside Dreams
programme.desc: A couple with a crew of rambunctious kids and a dream house in San Antonio is in desperate need of a pool. The kings design an expansive pool with a Baja shelf, swim-up bar, giant infinity edge and massive two-storey deck to enjoy the view.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Sausages, Franks and Dogs
programme.desc: In Sagaponack, N.Y., Michael Symon heads to a smokehouse where succulent, slow-smoked pork ribs are mopped with a tangy, citrus barbecue sauce. In Columbus, Ohio, there's a beefy burger topped with smoky kielbasa.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Bacon Behemoths
programme.desc: Michael Symon heads to Providence, where a traditional Rhode Island hot wiener in a crepe topped with chili is the ultimate local comfort food. In Indianapolis, he digs into a steak patty topped with Chinese char siu slaw.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Festival of Fries
programme.desc: Everyone's favorite side dish takes center stage as Noah Cappe celebrates over-the-top fries loaded with every topping you could imagine.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Aporkalypse Now
programme.desc: Noah visits the Great American Foodie Fest in Las Vegas and the Topfield Fair in Massachusetts.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: There's Something About Cherry
programme.desc: Noah Cappe arrives in Macon, Georgia, for the International Cherry Blossom Festival and some of the prettiest, pinkest carnival treats around.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Attack of the Mac and Cheese
programme.desc: Noah Cappe arrives at the Kentucky State Fair and gets a satisfying taste of all things Southern. He starts off with the Mac Ball Burger featuring deep-fried homemade mac and cheese.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Six Degrees of Bacon
programme.desc: Noah Cappe goes hog-wild with fantastic porky delights. Pork is as delicious as it is versatile, which allows these amazing carnival cooks to get creative. Whether it's grilled, smoked or even incorporated into dessert, it's time to pig out.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Super Sundaes
programme.desc: We all scream for ice cream, but when it comes to super sundaes, these carnival concessionaires have gone one step beyond, and host Noah Cappe is here to try them all!

programme.title: Man's Greatest Food: Pub Grub
programme.desc: Roger Mooking goes on a pub crawl to find 15 of the best dishes to go with any drink. Chicken wings, nachos, sliders, apps and mains, they're all here. Chefs share insider tips and tricks for making the very best, tastiest bar fare.

programme.title: Food Paradise: Donut Shop
programme.desc: We're frying a batch of donuts for every meal of the day starting off in Minneapolis with a bacon, egg and cheese breakfast sandwich made on a sweet glazed donut. Then, we're in Texas for donuts with brisket and finally we try donut burgers and soup.

programme.title: Food Paradise: Sandwich Obsession
programme.desc: Whether it's Southern barbecue on a bun, a Tex-Mex take on a banh mi or lasagna grilled cheese, any mixture of fillings between buttery buns makes for a dream come true. From savory to spicy to sweet, there are no limits to our sandwich obsession.

programme.title: Late Nite Eats: Denver
programme.desc: Chef Jordan Andino heads out west to explore the late night food scene in Denver. His first stop is to try some Colorado-inspired appetizers, including elk lettuce wraps and salmon tostadas.

programme.title: Late Nite Eats: Milwaukee
programme.desc: Jordan Andino is in Milwaukee to sample the city's cheesiest offerings. First up, he heads downtown for a beer cheese-laden Wisco Burger at Oak Barrel Public House. Then he's off to wine bar Balzac for a cheesy spin on a classic dessert.

programme.title: Man's Greatest Food: Cajun Cuisine
programme.desc: Roger Mooking showcases 15 of America's best Cajun dishes, from classics like crawfish, jambalaya and gumbo to a few surprises.

programme.title: Man's Greatest Food: Meat
programme.desc: Roger Mooking visits the most creative chefs in the U.S. to get their mouthwatering secrets for meaty celebrations that include whole hog barbecue, buffalo meatloaf and more.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Feast Over Flame
programme.desc: Visit Skylight Inn, family-run restaurant in North Carolina who have been serving whole hog-style barbecue since the mid 1800s.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Fire Trucks
programme.desc: Big Red Rig, a bright red, double-decker, thirty-foot trailer tricked out with two massive smokers cooking traditional pulled pork.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Fired-Up Chefs
programme.desc: Two chefs from Texas who love to play with smoke and fire in their restaurants. Tom Spaulding serves classic Texas-style barbecue at his restaurant Live Oak and Tim Byres rolls out spice-rubbed chickens from his unique wood-burning rotisserie.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Cowboy Cooking
programme.desc: Chicken fried steak over an open fire and peach cobbler in a cast iron skillet.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Pit Masters
programme.desc: In Lexington, Texas folks line up early on Saturday mornings for Snow's BBQ.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Monster Rigs
programme.desc: Bill Rousseau transformed a retired Cessna airplane into a smoker.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Meadowlands State Fair/ Gardendale Magnolia Festival
programme.desc: Noah Cappe samples the freak show food of New Jersey and tries southern sweep potato pie ice cream.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: California State Fair/ Northeast State Fair
programme.desc: Noah Cappe chows down on a lobster corn dog in California, and tries a deep fried peanut butter and banana sandwich in Pennsylvania.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Stanislaus County Fair/ Texas Crab Fest
programme.desc: Noah Cappe arrives at the Stanislaus County Fair in Turlock, California. He gets his paws into homemade fries topped with lobster claws and tail meat, and devours a delicious donut ice cream sandwich.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Hogs and Dogs
programme.desc: Noah Cappe sets sail for The Great Frederick Fair in Maryland, where he goes all in for The Whole Hog, finds his sea legs with The Crabby Dog, rounds out his plate with Cake-a-Dilla and dances for joy with the Hula Boat.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Fusion Fiesta
programme.desc: Noah Cappe lifts the lid on the American melting pot to reveal some decadent, delicious results. When fair goers meet fusion foods, the results can only be world domination.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Some Like It Popped
programme.desc: Noah hits the Big Butler Fair in Prospect, PA., where he tears into the Conevore, filled to the brim with smoked brisket, pork and chicken.

programme.title: Yum and Yummer: Southern Staples
programme.desc: Eddie Jackson knows that you just can't beat authentic Southern cooking. Whether it's hearty and savory breakfasts, traditional and delicious low-country cuisine or sweet desserts, it’s a full day of your favorite Southern staples.

programme.title: Yum and Yummer: Campus Cravings

programme.desc: Mulder rescues Scully after she is abducted by a deranged man who believes in UFOs.
